articles.title: SPOTIFY'S WEAR OS OVERHAUL BRINGS CLEANER VISUALS AND QUICKER CONTROLS TO YOUR WRIST
articles.summary: Spotify has launched a major visual overhaul of its Wear OS app featuring cleaner visuals, swipe-based navigation, and easier access to playback controls. The redesign includes a music-first mode that highlights album art, simplified tap-and-double-tap controls, and quick access to key sections like Home, Search, and Your Library. The update is rolling out via the Google Play Store over the coming days, with more features planned for future releases.

articles.title: DESIGN UI USING AI WITH STITCH FROM GOOGLE LABS
articles.summary: Google Labs has evolved Stitch into an AI-native design canvas that lets users create high-fidelity UI designs from natural-language descriptions via "vibe design." The platform features an infinite canvas, a design agent that reasons across entire projects, and voice capabilities for real-time collaboration and iteration. Stitch enables rapid prototyping by transforming static designs into interactive prototypes instantly and integrates with other workflow tools through its MCP server and SDK.

articles.title: YOU CAN NOW TEACH ADOBE FIREFLY TO GENERATE IMAGES IN YOUR OWN VISUAL STYLE
articles.summary: Adobe has launched Firefly custom models in public beta, allowing users to train AI on their own visual style using 10 to 30 images, enabling consistent generation of images that match their aesthetic across projects. The feature is available to premium subscribers and costs 500 credits per training, with support for photography, illustration, and character styles.

articles.title: DESIGN PROCESS ISN'T DEAD, IT'S COMPRESSED
articles.summary: The recent argument to abandon the design process in favor of intuition and rapid building misrepresents how experienced designers actually work. What appears to be "skipping process" is actually process compression - experienced designers internalize and adapt traditional frameworks, moving fluidly through discovery, ideation, and evaluation stages. Solution-first approaches only work effectively in mature problem spaces with established patterns, not when inventing something entirely new.

articles.title: GENERATIVE UI: USEFUL, OR JUST CHAOS WITH BETTER BRANDING?
articles.summary: Generative UI refers to interfaces dynamically created by AI in response to user input or context, rather than using fixed screens and components. While it offers benefits like personalization, reduced friction, and context-awareness, it risks undermining user confidence through unpredictability and breaking muscle memory. Success requires strict adherence to guardrails, including design system constraints, user control options, accessibility planning, and stable core navigation patterns.

articles.title: DESIGNERS, THERE'S NO EXCUSE FOR ACCESSIBILITY AS AN AFTERTHOUGHT
articles.summary: Accessible design is often overlooked in graphic design due to habits, assumptions, and late-stage implementation, but it should be a core part of the process because designing inclusively leads to better outcomes for everyone. Start accessibility early, consult people with lived experience, and use simple practices—like not relying on color alone—to make designs more effective and inclusive.

articles.title: AI VIDEO AD GENERATOR
articles.summary: HeyFish.ai is an AI-powered video ad platform that creates hyper-realistic digital human video ads.

articles.title: AI COMIC GENERATOR
articles.summary: Makecomics is an AI-powered comic generator that creates professional comic panels instantly. Users can describe their scene, choose from styles like Noir, American Modern, Manga, or Vintage.

articles.title: A DESIGNER'S THOUGHTS ABOUT THIS MOMENT IN AI
articles.summary: The concerning shift from academic AI research to rapid commercialization has created a reckless arms race. This trajectory is fundamentally opposed to the creators' mission of making the world better, as it injects powerful, unknown technology into systems designed to harm rather than help people. Designers and creators now face unprecedented tension between their desire to improve the world and the morally questionable origins of the AI tools they're pressured to use.

articles.title: FROM SYMBOLS TO SYSTEMS: DESIGNING BRANDS THAT HOLD UNDER PRESSURE
articles.summary: Strong brand systems fail when they're too rigid. Effective brands focus on a few core, non-negotiable elements (like logo, color, and typography) while allowing flexibility to adapt quickly to culture and new media. Resilient branding comes from balancing a stable “anchor” with creative freedom (“volume control”), continuously testing across contexts, and empowering teams to evolve the system rather than strictly follow rules.

articles.title: THE IDENTITY FOR SPATIAL FESTIVAL FEELS APTLY THREE-DIMENSIONAL
articles.summary: The Spatial festival at Funkhaus Berlin commissioned SMLXL and Gerard Mallandrich to create a dynamic, motion-led identity that reflects its immersive, experimental sound environments. By treating motion as the core of the design and building a responsive 3D visual space, the identity became a living, interactive extension of the festival experience—balancing avant-garde expression with clarity and accessibility.

articles.title: HOW WE TAUGHT AI TO UNDERSTAND LIGHT
articles.summary: Researchers taught AI to understand how light actually behaves rather than just imitating lighting patterns from images.

articles.title: JANNY BAEK'S CERAMICS LOOK LIKE THEY'RE STILL EVOLVING
articles.summary: Janny Baek's ceramic sculptures appear to be caught mid-transformation, resembling living organisms that haven't finished evolving.

articles.title: BOND CREATES A NEW BRAND STRATEGY AND IDENTITY FOR LAB MUOTOILUINSTITUUTTI
articles.summary: Helsinki consultancy BOND rebranded LAB Muotoiluinstituutti around the concept “The Reformers,” positioning it as a dynamic force that challenges design norms through a constantly evolving identity.
